title: festuca duriuscula subvar. cinerea
intro: Festuca duriuscula subvar. cinerea, also known as cinerea rough fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe and protect wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Festuca duriuscula subvar. cinerea include its ability to act as an expectorant, helping to clear mucus from the lungs, and its potential to reduce inflammation and improve circulation. In herbal preparations, Festuca duriuscula subvar. cinerea can be used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
